primary_diagnosis.name: Acute auricular flare of relapsing polychondritis; bacterial perichondritis/mastoiditis not fully excludable remotely

pathophysiology_summary: Relapsing polychondritis is an autoimmune inflammation of cartilage; the auricular form produces an acutely red, hot, swollen, tender ear that characteristically SPARES the earlobe, because the lobe contains no cartilage — a near-pathognomonic discriminator from bacterial perichondritis and cellulitis (which involve the whole ear including the lobe). It can be recurrent and self-limiting, but can also involve nasal, laryngotracheal (airway) and costal cartilage and the eyes. The alarming red hot ear raises fear of bacterial perichondritis (which threatens cartilage necrosis/cauliflower ear), mastoiditis (post-auricular swelling, ear protrusion, canal discharge) and intracranial spread. Although earlobe sparing strongly favours polychondritis, telehealth cannot palpate the mastoid or definitively exclude a co-existing cartilage infection, so urgent in-person assessment is prudent alongside NSAIDs, with a mastoiditis/airway safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Acute red hot tender ear with earlobe sparing on a known episodic-cartilage-inflammation background.
Threatens cartilage necrosis; usually involves the whole ear with a piercing/trauma history — earlobe sparing argues against it but it cannot be fully excluded remotely.
Screen for post-auricular swelling, ear protrusion, canal discharge, fever; absent.
Cellulitis involves the whole ear including the lobe, not just the cartilage.

Earlobe sparing (DI-001), afebrile with no post-auricular swelling/discharge/piercing (DI-002), no airway/eye involvement (DI-003) — most likely a polychondritis flare, but cartilage infection cannot be fully excluded remotely.
Cannot be fully excluded without examination; the reason for urgent in-person assessment.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: recognise earlobe sparing as pointing to polychondritis, but arrange urgent same/next-day in-person assessment (cartilage infection threatens the ear and cannot be excluded remotely), NSAIDs/cold compress interim, and a firm mastoiditis/airway safety-net. Neither home-only reassurance nor reflex ED without red flags is ideal.

red_flags.description: Bacterial perichondritis/mastoiditis — whole-ear/lobe involvement, post-auricular swelling with ear protrusion, canal discharge, or fever
red_flags.clinical_significance: Cartilage infection threatens necrosis; mastoiditis can spread intracranially.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Cartilage necrosis (cauliflower ear) or intracranial spread.

red_flags.description: Airway/other-cartilage relapsing polychondritis — hoarseness, tracheal pain, or breathing difficulty

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Reassuring home-only because of earlobe sparing, without arranging in-person assessment to exclude a co-existing cartilage infection.
Failing to screen/safety-net laryngotracheal relapsing polychondritis, which is life-threatening.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Urgent same/next-day in-person assessment (GP/ENT); NSAIDs + cold compress interim; firm mastoiditis/airway safety-net.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Home-only reassurance could miss a cartilage infection (cartilage necrosis) or evolving mastoiditis; over-triaging every polychondritis flare to ED without red flags is unnecessary.
